rationale_top3: Exceptional entertainment facilities with resort-style heated pool complex featuring two waterslides that keep children entertained for hours, complemented by jumping pillow, pedal karts, and daily kids activities coordinated by enthusiastic staff Outstanding cleanliness standards consistently described as 'spotless,' 'immaculate,' and 'the cleanest park' with amenities maintained to exceptional standards throughout the day, backed by dedicated cleaning staff Prime waterfront location providing direct access to coastal walks and beaches, plus walking distance to major supermarkets, cafes, restaurants and Caloundra town centre, combining natural beauty with urban convenience
rationale_honourable: Strong family loyalty with guests returning annually for 3-12+ years and rebooking before departure, demonstrating exceptional satisfaction and memorable experiences Spacious premium sites with well-maintained artificial grass and drive-through options, though site sizing varies across the property Beautiful natural setting with salt-water creek, ocean views from premium cabins, and easy access to regional attractions including Australia Zoo and Aussie World
water_fun: Heated pool complex, exciting water slides suitable for all ages, resort-style aquatic facilities
kids_play: Jumping pillow, pedal karts, go-karts, daily supervised kids activities with coordinator, movie nights, discos, bingo and trivia
pet_detail: Dogs allowed with $10 per night fee, however no dedicated dog facilities or off-leash areas mentioned in reviews
best_for: Best for families wanting waterslides, pools and easy beach access.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Caloundra Waterfront Holiday Park
SCORE: 87/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 440

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Exceptional heated pool complex with water slides described as "amazing" and suitable for all ages, plus jumping pillow, pedal karts, go-karts, daily kids activities with coordinator Nick praised by name, movie nights, discos, bingo, trivia, and live music events at the restaurant. Families report kids "having the time of their lives" and "never a dull moment."

Nature (16/20) — Prime waterfront location with "stunning walks along the coast just a step out of the park," direct beach access through back gate to coastal walking paths, views over Pumicestone Passage, beautiful landscaped gardens with native birds, and close proximity to beaches. Some reviewers note it's not directly beachfront and requires short walk to quality swimming beaches.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding cleanliness across all facilities with reviews consistently using terms like "spotless," "immaculate," "sparkling clean," and "the cleanest campground I have stayed at." Amenities, camp kitchen, pool area, and grounds maintained to "consistently high standard" with housekeeping staff (Louise, Sienna, Nikki, Jane, Bree) mentioned by name for exceptional work.

Value (11/15) — Mixed value perception; many families find it "worth every cent" for the resort-style experience, excellent facilities, and location. Restaurant described as "reasonably priced" with "great value." However, significant concerns about pricing with multiple reviews noting it's "the most expensive park we have stayed in" at $100+ per powered site, cabin pricing $350-400/night seen as excessive by some, and recent price increases ($800 jump year-on-year noted) causing affordability concerns for repeat visitors.

Site Size (7/10) — Sites described as "adequate" and "reasonable size, not large but very adequate" with "plenty of space." Premium sites on artificial grass are "spacious" and "well maintained," but standard sites vary significantly—some cramped with "vans hanging over onto the road," tight maneuvering, and cars parked on roadways. Drive-through sites praised but some tent/camper sites described as "TINY" with space concerns.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional guest sentiment with overwhelming majority of 5-star reviews, high recommendation rate, and numerous repeat visitors ("3rd year in a row," "4th time here," "12 years coming back"). Guests express strong emotional connection: "feels like coming home," "can't wait to come back," "already rebooked for next year." Staff culture praised with guests noting staff "clearly enjoyed working there."

Location (10/10) — Perfect location "right on the water" with "everything within walking distance"—Coles and Woolworths "2 minute walk," cafes, restaurants, RSL, shops, and beaches all accessible on foot. Coastal boardwalk directly outside back gate, Caloundra town center easily reached, and convenient base for regional attractions (Australia Zoo, Aussie World, Eumundi Markets) all within easy drive.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Value concerns are significant: pricing at $100+ per site and $350+ per cabin is substantially higher than comparable parks, with recent sharp price increases causing affordability issues for families and loyal repeat customers who report the park is "getting out of reach."

2. Site size and placement inconsistency: while premium sites are excellent, many standard sites are cramped with tight access, road noise issues (particularly along Bulcock Street with traffic noise "constant until midnight"), and significant variation in quality between different areas of the park.

3. Minor facility gaps: only one key provided for amenity access (multiple families note this as problematic), pool closes at 6:30pm (earlier than desired), limited kids playground equipment (jumping pillow only, no traditional playground mentioned), no dedicated dog facilities despite $10/night pet fee, and some maintenance issues noted (decking with protruding nails, BBQ hire equipment underperforming).

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Simply the Best Caravan park in Queensland," "cleanest campground I have stayed at," "feels like staying at a luxury resort," "pool and slides provide hours of fun," "staff went above and beyond," "immaculate amenities and facilities," "everything within walking distance," "the best camp kitchen I have seen"

rationale_top3: Exceptional family entertainment facilities including heated pool with water slides, jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal karts, and daily organized activities such as kids' discos, movie nights, archery, and wildlife shows that keep children engaged throughout their stay. Beautiful Maroochy River frontage providing natural setting with fishing, kayaking, crabbing from the pontoon, and stunning sunset views, combined with extremely spacious powered sites described as the biggest guests have experienced. Resort-style experience with luxury glamping safari tents featuring full bathrooms and air conditioning, poolside cabana bookings, food service, and consistently praised staff who go above and beyond to create a welcoming family atmosphere.
rationale_honourable: Convenient Sunshine Coast location just 15 minutes from major attractions like the Big Pineapple and Sea Life Aquarium, with easy highway access for day trips to beaches and Noosa, while maintaining well-maintained facilities that most guests found clean and spotless.
water_fun: Heated pool with two waterslides, splash pool
kids_play: Playground, jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal karts
pet_detail: Dog friendly with leash requirements; dogs permitted in designated areas including bar/bistro ground floor
best_for: Best for families wanting a resort-style holiday with waterslides and activities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Ingenia Holidays Rivershore
SCORE: 85/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-23
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 302

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently praise the heated pool with two "ridiculously fun" waterslides, organized daily activities including archery, animal encounters, kids discos, movie nights, scavenger hunts, and facilities like mini golf, pedal karts, jumping pillow, and playground. Families report children being entertained all day with "so much to do" and many declare it "one of the best holiday parks ever."

Nature (16/20) — Located directly on Maroochy River with beautiful waterfront sites, fishing access from pontoon and jetty, and "stunning views of sunsets and sunrise." Reviewers appreciate the "peaceful natural setting," bird life, and proximity to beaches (15 mins) and attractions like Australia Zoo. Large grassy areas and "spectacular well manicured grounds" create a resort-in-nature feel.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Facilities described as "spotless," "immaculate," and "very clean" with modern amenities throughout. Multiple camp kitchens, clean showers with "great water pressure," and regular maintenance noted. Some reviews mention occasional lapses during peak periods with amenities needing more frequent cleaning, and a few specific maintenance issues like gravel on grass sites and water drainage from pool showers.

Value (12/15) — Pricing generates mixed feedback with powered sites ranging $87-250+ per night and glamping tents $580+ per night. Many find it "well worth it" for the resort-style experience and facilities, while others feel it's "expensive" or "over priced" compared to what's offered. Positive value mentions include reasonable food/drink prices, happy hour specials, and "great value for money" on glamping accommodation.

Site Size (9/10) — Sites consistently described as "huge," "massive," "extremely spacious," and "the biggest site I've ever stated at." Unpowered riverfront sites are "HUGE" and powered sites fit large rigs easily. Reviewers note "plenty of room" even with 30ft caravans and that glamping tents feel very private with gardens/trees between them.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with families declaring "we will definitely be back," "already booked for next year," and "can't wait to come back." Staff repeatedly praised as "amazing," "friendly," and "going out of their way." Return visitors common with some visiting 3-4 times. Minor detractors include occasional rude staff encounters, road noise complaints, and crowding during peak periods with day visitors.

Location (8/10) — Central Sunshine Coast position with "good location for day trips." Approximately 15 mins to beaches, Big Pineapple, and SEA LIFE Aquarium; 7km from Maroochydore; 4km to Bli Bli shopping. Riverfront setting praised but positioned on busy David Low Way creating road noise. Described as "blissful" being slightly out of town yet convenient to everything.

WHY NOT 100:
Road noise from David Low Way is a significant complaint with multiple reviews mentioning traffic noise "all night" that disrupted sleep and "destroyed all the beauty" for some tent campers. Peak period overcrowding impacts experience with day visitors (paying $20 entry) filling the pool, cabanas, and restaurant to capacity while overnight guests paying $200-580+ per night struggle to find seating or pool space. Cleanliness and maintenance standards show inconsistency with some reviews noting facilities not cleaned daily during busy periods, gravel spread over grass sites, drainage issues creating muddy areas after rain, and specific maintenance lapses like blood on toilet doors and cobwebs remaining for days.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"ridiculously fun slides", "one of the best holiday parks ever", "absolute paradise for families", "staff went above and beyond", "kids never stopped smiling", "glamping at its best", "heated pool perfect for cooler nights", "spectacular and well manicured grounds", "everything you need", "resort style feeling", "VERY noisy at night", "traffic noise all night", "nearly $200 a night I would expect staff to be a bit friendlier", "over capacity for enjoyable use of facilities"

park_name: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park
rationale_top3:
Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park earns Gold status with exceptional cleanliness, outstanding location, and consistent family-friendly excellence. Reviews repeatedly praise the "spotlessly clean" cabins and amenities, "immaculate" grounds, and the park's prime position just a 5-minute walk from a patrolled beach with cafes, shops, and restaurants all within easy reach. The heated pool, playground, and coffee van add genuine family value.

Families particularly appreciate the spacious, well-maintained sites with privacy hedges, excellent water pressure in showers, and genuinely friendly, helpful staff who go "above and beyond." The park's central Sunshine Coast location makes it perfect for exploring Mooloolaba and Maroochydore, while the adjacent lake and parkland provide peaceful natural surroundings. With many guests reporting repeat visits over 10-15 years, the consistent quality and value shine through.
rationale_honourable: The park's exceptional nature score reflects its enviable position between beach and lake, with beautiful palm tree surroundings and an amazing coastal walkway. Outstanding sentiment scores demonstrate genuine guest loyalty with repeat visitors spanning 10-15 years who consistently rebook for the same time each year. Strong value for money, particularly the "stay 7 nights, pay for 6" deal, makes this an affordable family option despite premium cleanliness and location standards.
pet_detail: Dog friendly on caravan sites (not in cabins), nearby lake walk and beach are dog-friendly on-leash
best_for: Best for families wanting a beachside location and a quieter holiday.
executive_summary:
PARK: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 252

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews mention a heated pool that's "great for kids," a playground described as "cute little playground," and a games room, though some noted the arcade room "was a let down" needing upgrades. Coffee van and visiting food trucks (especially wood-fired pizza) add family convenience. Limited structured kids' activities or entertainment programs mentioned.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional beach access with "5 minute walk to patrolled beach," backing onto "lovely parkland" and a "pet friendly walk around lake full of bird life." Guests loved the "beautiful palm tree surroundings" and "amazing coastal walkway." The natural setting between beach and lake is a major drawcard.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding cleanliness across the board: "spotlessly clean," "immaculate grounds," "amenities were modern, spotlessly clean," "meticulously clean," and "facilities always clean and very well maintained" are consistent themes. Perfect score reflects exceptional standards.

Value (14/15) — Strong value proposition with "excellent value for money," "great value powered sites," "reasonable prices," and "affordable" pricing noted frequently. The "stay 7 nights, pay for 6" deal appeals to families. Minor deductions for some guests finding it "expensive" or "stiff price."

Site Size (9/10) — Very positive feedback on "excellent sized caravan sites," "spacious sites," "roomy" with "plenty of room to move around," and privacy hedges between sites. A few noted "not a heap of space" and cabins can be "small" or "cramped" for larger groups.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional guest sentiment with countless repeat visitors (10-15 years), many rebooked "for same time next year," "will definitely be back," and "wouldn't keep coming back if it wasn't exceptional." Staff praised as "friendly," "helpful," going "above and beyond."

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score: "great location," "walking distance to beach, cafes, shops," "central to everything on the Sunshine Coast," close to Maroochydore and Mooloolaba, yet "nice and quiet" set back from main roads. Ideal positioning.

WHY NOT 100:
On-site entertainment is limited with basic playground facilities and a games room that disappointed some families expecting more activities — no waterpark, jumping pillow, or organized kids' programs mentioned. Some cabins and sites are quite compact with guests noting "small" spaces, cramped rooms for 3-4 people, and tiny bathrooms that felt restrictive. A handful of negative experiences included maintenance issues (faulty appliances, holes in screens, dirty rooms), refund disputes, and reports of poor customer service responses to complaints, though these were exceptions to generally excellent feedback.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean", "5 minute walk to the beach", "friendly and helpful staff", "immaculate grounds", "great location", "heated pool", "privacy hedges between sites", "excellent water pressure", "coffee van on site", "family friendly atmosphere", "will definitely be back", "couldn't be more helpful"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with amenities cleaned twice daily and described as 'spotless' across 416 reviews, earning perfect 15/15 cleanliness score Outstanding natural riverfront setting with direct access to Maroochy River walking/cycling paths, fishing spots, and beautiful tropical gardens earning 16/20 nature score Strong entertainment program for young families including jumping pillow, mini golf, school holiday activities, Wednesday pancake breakfasts, and evening campfires with marshmallows earning 14/20
rationale_honourable: The park's friendly, helpful staff consistently go 'above and beyond' with personalized service, while unique conveniences like the beloved 'marriage saver' tractor parking assistance create a welcoming community atmosphere that generates exceptional repeat visitation and loyal guests returning for 7+ years
water_fun: Riverside location on the Maroochy River providing natural water access and ambience for water-based activities
kids_play: Strong entertainment score indicates excellent children's facilities and activities, making this a standout choice for families with kids
pet_detail: Moderate pet score (7/20) suggests pets are accommodated with some restrictions or limited pet-friendly areas
best_for: Best for families wanting a relaxed riverside holiday close to attractions.

rationale_top3: Outstanding natural setting with nature score of 19/20, indicating excellent coastal environment and natural beauty Strong entertainment facilities scoring 14/20, suggesting good amenities and activities for families High cleanliness standards at 13/20, demonstrating well-maintained facilities and grounds
rationale_honourable: Excellent location score of 10/10, positioned at Dicky Beach on the Sunshine Coast with premium beach access Good value for money at 11/20, offering reasonable pricing for the coastal location and facilities provided
water_fun: Pool onsite, several want it bigger

executive_summary:
PARK: Dicky Beach Family Holiday Park
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 133

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews mention pool, tennis courts, games room, rec room, and playground positioned next to BBQ area for supervision. Pizza nights and food vans occasionally visit. However, multiple reviews note entertainment is modest compared to other parks, with comments like "not really a kids park as caravan parks go these days" and requests for "a bigger pool or second pool." School holiday activities mentioned positively by some families.

Nature (19/20) — Exceptional beach access is the standout feature with "direct beach access," "patrolled beach," "right on the beach," and "only a carpark away" appearing repeatedly. Dicky Beach Surf Club adjacent. Coastal walking tracks north and south. Many mature trees provide shade over sites. SS Dicky shipwreck remnants on beach. Minor deduction for urban setting rather than bushland.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Majority praise cleanliness with "immaculately clean," "amenities always clean," "spotless," and "very well maintained" appearing frequently. However, two serious incidents reported: gastro outbreak with "poo smeared in toilet bowls for half the day" and management's poor response, plus complaints about insufficient toilet facilities ("only 3 cubicles for 75 sites") causing queues. Generally excellent but inconsistencies noted.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on value. Positive comments include "excellent value for money," "reasonable prices," and "good value for money." However, multiple recent reviews cite significant price increases: cabin costs rose from "$760 to $1200 per week," "prices increase every time," and "$131 for 2 nights for powered site was expensive." One long-term guest stated they "will never be coming back because of new pricing."

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive with "spacious sites," "large caravan sites," "plenty of room to move," and "big sites with plenty of room." Some reviews mention sites are "little tight getting van in" and powered sites described as "sehr klein" (very small) and close to neighbors. Unpowered sites noted as more spacious under trees.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong positive sentiment overall with numerous "will definitely be back," "highly recommend," and repeat visitors including one stating "10th year at this park." Some families visit annually. However, negative experiences regarding staff attitude, poor customer service responses, a concerning child safety incident, and pricing concerns temper the perfect score.

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score with universal praise. "Prime location," "perfect location," "location location location" repeated throughout. Direct beach access, shops/IGA/cafes/restaurants across the road, surf club next door, bus stops nearby, skatepark out front, and close to Glass House Mountains for day trips. "Everything needed within walking distance."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Entertainment facilities are adequate but modest for a family holiday park, with several reviews noting it's "not really a kids park" compared to modern standards and requests for more/bigger pool facilities reducing the entertainment score.

2. Two serious cleanliness incidents (gastro outbreak with poor management response, and persistent toilet facility inadequacy with long queues) alongside staffing/customer service complaints prevent a perfect cleanliness and operations score despite generally excellent standards.

3. Significant and repeated price increases have impacted value perception, with cabin prices jumping 58% and multiple guests stating they won't return due to costs, particularly when compared to renting houses nearby for less money.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"right on the beach," "immaculately clean and very well maintained," "playground right next to BBQ area so you can watch your kids," "patrolled beach," "shops across the road," "spacious sites," "security guard on patrol," "location location location," "toilet queues for 75 sites," "price increases every time," "not really a kids park," "fantastic park for children"

rationale_top3: Outstanding natural environment with direct access to both patrolled surf beach and calm Maroochy River inlet, consistently praised as 'best of both worlds' with safe swimming for children Exceptional location described as 'you can't beat' with walking distance to Cotton Tree village, cafes, restaurants, shops, and Maroochydore CBD Generally well-maintained facilities with many recent reviews noting 'spotless' and 'impeccably clean' amenities when not at peak capacity
rationale_honourable: Cotton Tree Holiday Park earns Silver classification primarily for its exceptional beachfront location between the Maroochy River inlet and patrolled surf beach. While the natural setting is world-class and facilities are generally well-maintained, the park is let down by cramped sites, inconsistent customer service, premium pricing that doesn't match amenities offered, and lack of on-site entertainment facilities like pools or playgrounds that families expect at this price point.
water_fun: No pool; beach and river access only
kids_play: No playground or structured kids entertainment
best_for: Best for families wanting calm water swimming and a central location.
executive_summary:
PARK: Cotton Tree Holiday Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 257

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note the complete absence of onsite kids entertainment, playground equipment, or pool facilities. Families mention "no pool or other entertainment" and "not much to offer except a beach." The park relies entirely on natural beach and river access for recreation, with occasional food trucks mentioned as the only organized activity.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting with reviewers praising "perfect location on the ocean and inlet," "right between the river and the beach," and "crystal clear calm waterways great for kids." Direct access to both patrolled surf beach and calm Maroochy River inlet provides versatile swimming options. Multiple reviews highlight "amazing sunrises," "beautiful beach sunrises," and "stunning sunsets" as standout natural features.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Generally positive cleanliness feedback with frequent mentions of "spotless amenities," "impeccably clean," and "cleanest caravan park toilet I ever been." However, recent reviews note deterioration during peak periods: "amenities were gross," "toilets and showers were dirty," and "amenities beyond filth" during busy school holidays. Cleaning schedules at 8am receive criticism for inconveniencing early risers.

Value (8/15) — Significant concerns about pricing with multiple reviews calling it "very expensive," "overpriced," and "far too expensive." Specific complaints include "$76-$84 per night with no pool or entertainment," "$80 a night is outrageous," and "most expensive nightly rate" compared to parks with more facilities. Families feel the premium charged is purely for location, not amenities or site quality.

Site Size (5/10) — Major recurring complaint across reviews: "sites are really small and close together," "crammed in like sardines," "everyone packed really tightly in," and "way too small." Multiple families report difficulty maneuvering larger vans with "20+ minutes to squeeze our van in" and "had to leave due to not fitting into allocated sites." Corner and waterfront sites receive slightly better ratings.

Sentiment (8/10) — Mixed sentiment with strong location loyalty balanced by management frustrations. Long-term visitors ("coming for 16-20 years") express disappointment with new ownership: "staff are so unfriendly," "rigid enforcement of rules," and "rude staff, awful communication." However, many still return for the location: "our favorite park to stay at," "will definitely be back," and "already booked for next year."

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for location with universal praise: "best location on the sunny coast," "walking distance to cafes and shops," "everything at your fingertips," and "close to Maroochydore CBD." Direct beach access, proximity to Cotton Tree village shops/cafes across the road, and central Sunshine Coast position consistently rated as the park's strongest asset.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities (no pool, playground, or kids activities) makes this purely a beach-access park rather than a full holiday park experience, with families specifically noting "no pool or other entertainment which other parks have."

2. Severely cramped site sizing causes significant stress and safety concerns, with multiple families unable to fit their vans, cars parked blocking roads, and children's safety compromised by tight spacing: "tragic accident waiting to happen because of cars parked blocking vision."

3. Substantial decline in customer service quality under current management, with numerous recent reviews documenting rude staff interactions, inflexible policies, unauthorized credit card charges, poor communication, and dismissive attitudes toward loyal long-term guests, creating a "cult of unhappiness" atmosphere that undermines the beautiful location.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Perfect location on the ocean and inlet," "sites are really small and close together," "crammed in like sardines," "no pool or other entertainment," "staff are so unfriendly," "very expensive to say the least," "amazing sunrises at your doorstep," "clean amenities and well maintained," "beautiful location, premium price," "right next to the river and the beach," "far too expensive," "best location on the sunny coast"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with direct access to Coolum's patrolled beach, dog-friendly beach zones, and walking distance to shops, Coles, restaurants, and surf club - universally praised as the park's strongest asset Consistently clean and well-maintained amenities with most reviews describing facilities as 'super clean,' 'spotless,' and 'always serviced,' demonstrating strong operational standards Outstanding natural environment backing onto beautiful sandy beach with multiple walking paths, providing an ideal coastal setting for families who prioritize beach access over manufactured entertainment
rationale_honourable: Coolum Beach Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its exceptional beachfront location on the Sunshine Coast, with direct access to patrolled swimming areas and dog-friendly beach zones. Families appreciate the spotless amenities, well-maintained grounds, and the convenience of walking to shops, cafes, and the surf club. While the park lacks pools, playgrounds, and organized kids' activities, the natural beach environment and proximity to everything needed makes it a solid choice for families who prioritize location and beach access over on-site entertainment facilities.
water_fun: No pool; beach access directly from park
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities on-site
pet_detail: Dog-friendly sites available in designated areas (approximately half the park); $5 per dog per night fee; direct access to dog-friendly beach areas
best_for: Best for families wanting direct beach access and a premium location.
executive_summary:
PARK: Coolum Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-05-29
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 173

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews consistently note there is no pool, no kids playground, no organized activities, and no entertainment facilities on-site. One reviewer stated "no pool or kids playground you have to go out of the park for that" and another noted "doesn't offer any extra touches to make the park special, like evening activities." The park relies entirely on the beach and external attractions for entertainment.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural environment with direct beach access being the standout feature. Reviews repeatedly praise "right on the beach," "perfect spot right across from the beach," "dog friendly beach access," and "beautiful long sandy beach." Multiple families mention the patrolled beach, surf areas, and dog-friendly sections. The coastal location with creek access for dogs and beachfront sites receives consistent praise.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Generally excellent with reviews frequently mentioning "super clean," "amenities very clean," "spotless amenities," and "facilities are excellent & clean as a whistle." However, some reviews mention declining standards with "slab was filthy on arrival," "amenities could be cleaner," and occasional maintenance issues like broken lights and security doors.

Value (8/15) — Significant concerns about pricing throughout reviews. Common phrases include "overpriced," "daylight robbery," "way too expensive," "$76-90 per night," "most expensive park we have stayed at," and complaints about additional fees like "$10 tractor fee," "$5 per dog per night," and "$10 charge to place van on site." Many note "no Pool and no wifi" doesn't justify the premium pricing.

Site Size (6/10) — Major recurring issue with "sites are too small for large vans," "very tight to get a caravan into the site," "sites are a bit cramped," "impossible to manoeuvre a caravan," and frequent need for "tractor to park vans." Some sites described as "about the width of a footpath" with "narrow roadways" and "high gutters." However, some premium beachfront sites received praise for generous space.

Sentiment (8/10) — Mixed sentiment with many loyal repeat visitors loving the location, but increasing dissatisfaction with pricing, staff, and facilities. Positive: "been coming here for years," "one of our favourite," "will definitely be returning." Negative: "won't be going back," "very disappointed," recurring complaints about rude staff, particularly reception, and feeling the park has "turned quite commercial."

Location (9/10) — Outstanding location consistently praised as the park's strongest feature. Reviews emphasize "walking distance to shops," "Coles, cafes and restaurants across the road," "close to beach, surf club," "handy to everything," and "perfect location right between beach and shopping strip." Accessibility to amenities without driving is highly valued.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities (no pool, no playground, no kids' club, no activities) - a critical gap for family holiday parks that significantly impacts the entertainment score and overall appeal for families with young children.
2. Serious site accessibility and size issues with many sites too tight for caravans, requiring paid tractor assistance, narrow laneways, and reports of damaged vans during parking - creating stress and additional costs for families.
3. Widespread and recurring complaints about staff attitude, particularly reception being "rude," "unhelpful," "abusive," and "not interested in customer service," combined with poor communication (phones not answered, no call-backs) which severely impacts the family experience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best beach and walking distance to everything," "super clean amenities," "sites are too small for large vans," "overpriced," "staff are very rude," "no pool or kids playground," "right on the beach," "most expensive park we have stayed at"

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachside location with walking distance to beach, cafes, park and dog-friendly beach access - the park's greatest strength with families consistently praising the 'beautiful beach on the sunny coast' and '2 minutes from the beach' convenience Exceptional nature score (18/20) reflecting the natural coastal setting, proximity to Maroochy River, and off-leash dog beach areas that create an ideal environment for outdoor-focused families Generally spacious, well-kept sites with amenities often described as 'the cleanest I've experienced at a van park ever' and 'impeccably clean', showing strong facility maintenance in core areas
rationale_honourable: Mudjimba Beach Holiday Park achieves Silver classification (68/100) through its premium beachside location and natural setting, with families returning 'year after year' for the unbeatable beach access and dog-friendly policies. However, significant issues prevent Gold status: severe cleanliness inconsistency (spotless amenities versus 'filthy' camp kitchens with cockroaches and mold), persistent reception staff attitude problems described as 'rude' and 'aggressive', minimal on-site kids entertainment (no playground on property, basic pool only), and premium pricing ($80-$100/night) that families feel doesn't match the value delivered compared to competitor parks with better facilities.

PARK: Mudjimba Beach Holiday Park

DATE SCORED: 2026-05-10
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 143

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — No on-site playground or kids club facilities mentioned. Families rely on playground across the road, pool, and beach for activities. Several reviews note lack of facilities compared to BIG 4 parks for the price. Food vans occasionally visit. Kids bring bikes to ride around park.

Nature (18/20) — Excellent beach location with walking distance access (2-3 minutes). Dog-friendly beach nearby. Close to cafes and natural environment. Shaded sites under paperbark trees. Bike paths available. Some mosquito issues in back sections near reserve.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Mixed feedback with modern, clean amenities praised in many recent reviews, but significant complaints about filthy camp kitchens (dirty fridges, cockroaches, moldy equipment). Amenities "impeccably clean" per some, but consistency varies by cleaning staff. Grounds maintenance issues with leaf debris.

Value (8/15) — Major pain point with widespread complaints about pricing. $80-$100 per night described as "over the top," "unethically high," and "way overpriced." 26% price increase without facility improvements noted. Dog fee of $5/night seen as money grab. Many say not value for money compared to alternatives.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive with "spacious" and "generous" sites mentioned. Drive-through options for big vans. Some poor sites (like site 1, 188) that are uneven or cramped. Grass sites advertised but dirt patches allocated. Sites close together in some areas.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitor base with families returning yearly, but sentiment declining in recent reviews. Many 5-star reviews offset by growing frustration with staff attitudes, pricing, and cleanliness. Loyal customers appreciated over new guests. Management responsiveness poor.

Location (8/10) — Excellent proximity to beach (2-3 min walk), cafes, restaurants within 5 min walk. Close to Coles, zoo, and Sunshine Coast attractions. Airport noise noted but manageable (no late flights). Walking distance to all amenities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Persistent staff attitude problems with multiple reviews citing rude, unhelpful reception staff who lack interpersonal skills and make guests feel unwelcome rather than greeting them warmly.
2. No dedicated on-site children's entertainment, playground, or activities — families must rely entirely on external beach/playground for kids, limiting appeal for families with young children seeking park-based activities.
3. Significant cleanliness failures in camp kitchens with cockroaches, moldy equipment, dirty facilities, and inconsistent maintenance that contradict the premium pricing and create health concerns.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"playground across the road for the kids," "Amenities are impeccably clean," "Filthy Kitchen by the Pool," "woman in reception needs an attitude adjustment," "way overpriced for quality," "families come back year after year," "walking distance to beach, cafes and park," "dog friendly beach just across the road"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachside location with 5-minute walk to patrolled beach, shops, restaurants and Mooloolaba attractions - rated 10/10 for location being 'one of the best on the Sunshine Coast' Consistently spotlessly clean amenities and well-maintained grounds scoring 14/15 for cleanliness, though recent reviews note some maintenance decline with broken equipment and less frequent grounds care Small, secure 60-site family-oriented park encouraging bikes, scooters and skateboards with concrete slabs and adequate site spacing, though completely lacking pool or on-site entertainment facilities
rationale_honourable: Mooloolaba Beach Holiday Park delivers on its prime beachside location with walking access to Mooloolaba's attractions, patrolled beach, cafes and shops. The park consistently receives praise for spotlessly clean amenities and well-maintained grounds, though families should note the absence of a pool and limited on-site entertainment. While the small 60-site park offers security, concrete slabs and basic facilities, value concerns and increasing prices are frequently mentioned by returning guests.
water_fun: Direct access to Mooloolaba Beach for swimming, surfing, and beach activities; natural ocean-based water recreation rather than park facilities
kids_play: Limited dedicated children's entertainment and play facilities on-site; beach and natural environment provide main activity options
pet_detail: Pets are not permitted at this holiday park
best_for: Best for families wanting to stay steps from one of Australia's best beaches.

rationale_top3: Exceptional natural setting with wooded environment, central lagoon/pond featuring ducks, turtles, water dragons, and eels that children love feeding - consistently praised as the park's standout feature Outstanding location just 500m from Australia Zoo with walking distance to Landsborough town, 20 minutes to beaches, and convenient access to Glasshouse Mountains and hinterland attractions Spacious powered sites with drive-through options and generally clean amenities, plus helpful staff creating a comfortable base for Sunshine Coast exploration
rationale_honourable: Landsborough Holiday Park earns its Silver rating primarily through its exceptional natural setting with pond, wildlife, and proximity to Australia Zoo. Families consistently praise the tranquil wooded environment where kids can observe ducks, turtles, water dragons, and abundant birdlife. The park's clean amenities, spacious powered sites, and helpful staff create a solid base for visiting Sunshine Coast attractions, though limited on-site entertainment and some maintenance issues prevent a higher score.
water_fun: Very small, dated pool needing renovation
kids_play: No playground facilities for kids reported
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with pets welcome on sites
best_for: Best for budget-conscious families visiting Australia Zoo.
executive_summary:
PARK: Landsborough Holiday Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 123

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews consistently highlight a critical gap: "Pool super small and no play ground area for kids," "no catering whatsoever to kids," and "the pool is tiny and old looks like the paints all coming off it." Multiple families noted the pool needs renovation and there are no playground or structured activities. One cricket team found staff accommodating by creating a pitch, but no dedicated kids' entertainment exists.

Nature (16/20) — Strong natural setting praised across reviews: "wooded area, felt nice to be in a natural surrounding," "kids loved the ducks and turtles in the pond," "lots of wildlife to see in the pond," "water dragons," "beautiful birds," and "little creek running behind" sites. The lagoon/pond with abundant birdlife is a consistent highlight. However, some noted "it stinks like the pond," and "smell was sickening" from duck droppings.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Mixed feedback with recent concerns. Positive: "Clean ammenities," "beautiful and clean," "SO CLEAN," "facilities were clean." However, multiple recent reviews note decline: "Has gotten mouldy in showers not cleaned as much," "Filthy in corners," "facilities were not clean, No hand soap," "floors had been swished with a dirty mop," and "toilets could do with refurbishment and a deep clean."

Value (9/15) — Several families questioned value: "over priced," "completely overpriced for what we got" ($55 unpowered), "$48 a night for a van site is a bit too expensive," "price should definitely be lower for what you get i feel ripped off honestly." Some found it reasonable: "Great value," "Affordable price," "Good value for money," but negative value comments are weighted more heavily in recent reviews.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive: "Powered sites were spacious," "generous sites easy access," "plenty of room for a larger van," "good sized camping sites," "Camping sites are fairly good size." Some concerns: "Powered sites small," "cabin was so little for family of 4," and gravel sites noted as not tent-friendly.

Sentiment (6/10) — Sentiment is mixed. Many positive: "will definitely be back," "would stay again," "highly recommend." However, significant negative experiences impact families: "permanent residents ruined this one for us," "didn't feel like we could really enjoy our caravan park," "permanent resident were verbal abusing us," "Never stay again," "would not stay again," "useless as a holiday park." Several families with active kids had poor experiences.

Location (9/10) — Excellent proximity to Australia Zoo praised extensively: "Perfect distance if you are heading to Australia Zoo. 4 mins away," "only 500 metres down the road," "5min drive to zoo," "courtesy shuttle bus to Australia Zoo." "Central location to explore the Sunshine coast," "walking distance to Landsborough town." Minor negatives: "beside a major intersection," "trains honking all night."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Critically inadequate family entertainment infrastructure: no playground, very small and dated pool "with paint coming off," no kids' activities or programs, making it unsuitable as a destination family holiday park.
2. Significant permanent resident conflicts limiting children's freedom: multiple families reported being told kids "weren't welcomed," couldn't ride scooters or play freely, with residents complaining about normal childhood activity, creating an uncomfortable environment for young families.
3. Declining cleanliness standards and maintenance issues: recent reviews note mouldy showers, filthy corners, dirty cleaning equipment, broken stoves, power outages without compensation, and facilities needing refurbishment, indicating maintenance has slipped.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Pool super small and no play ground area for kids", "kids loved the ducks and turtles in the pond", "only 500 metres down the road from Australia Zoo", "permanent residents weren't happy he was just running", "no catering whatsoever to kids", "over priced", "Clean ammenities, friendly staff", "Has gotten mouldy in showers not cleaned as much"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of kids entertainment facilities makes this unsuitable for families with young children, resulting in explicit warnings from family reviewers Inconsistent and declining cleanliness standards with multiple reports of disgusting amenities, missed cleaning schedules, pest infestations in cabins, and poor maintenance Extremely poor value proposition charging premium beachfront rates ($70-80/night) for run-down, aging facilities with minimal amenities that don't justify the cost
rationale_honourable: Maroochydore Beach Holiday Park benefits enormously from its outstanding beachfront location with direct private access, making it incredibly convenient for families who prioritise beach proximity above all else. The natural coastal environment is beautiful and the location near Cotton Tree and Mooloolaba offers excellent walkability to cafes, shops and attractions. However, the park falls short on family-specific facilities with no kids entertainment, inconsistent cleanliness standards, cramped sites, and pricing that doesn't reflect the aging infrastructure and minimal amenities provided.
water_fun: No pool or water facilities available on-site
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities available
pet_detail: Dogs allowed on site and beach on lead, $5 extra per night
best_for: Beach-focused adults and self-sufficient families who value prime beachfront location with 1-minute beach access over park facilities and can overlook maintenance inconsistencies
executive_summary:
PARK: Maroochydore Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-11
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 53

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (3/20) — Multiple families explicitly stated there is "nothing kid friendly or anything for kids to do except walk to the beach" with one review rating it "0/10" for families with children. No playground, pool, or organized activities mentioned across all reviews. One older reviewer noted "very little for children, which makes it great for oldies."

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional beach access with "private access gate" reaching beach "in 1 minute" consistently praised. Direct beachfront location with patrolled surf club nearby. Some sites feature tree coverage, though many described as open air. Walking distance to Cotton Tree and natural coastal environment highly valued.

Cleanliness (8/15) — Highly inconsistent across timeframes. Recent 2024-2026 reviews frequently cite "disgusting" amenities, "dirty" facilities, and one guest reported amenities not cleaned on four occasions over 13 nights with "showers a disgusting mess." One cabin had cockroaches and ants. However, some 2023-2025 reviews praised "spotless amenities" and "extraordinarily clean" facilities, suggesting staff turnover issues.

Value (6/15) — Overwhelming criticism of pricing: "Very expensive for what you get," "NOT worth 80$ per night," "very overpriced" appear repeatedly. Rates of $68.50-$80 per night cited as excessive given minimal facilities. Multiple reviews recommend competing Big 4 and Ingenia parks as better value.

Site Size (5/10) — Significant criticism of "EXTREAMLY tight and squishy" sites. Multiple reviews mention cramped conditions, though some guests with larger rigs reported adequate space. Sites described as variable in quality with some exposed to wind and road noise.

Sentiment (6/10) — Deeply polarized. Recent 2026 reviews heavily negative (multiple 1-star ratings) while older 2023-2024 reviews more positive. Several families stated they would "never recommend" or "won't be back." Some repeat guests booked for 2026, but overall trajectory declining. Emergency response failure and poor customer service damaged reputation.

Location (10/10) — Universally praised as "prime location," "central to everything," walking distance to shops, restaurants, Mooloolaba, and Cotton Tree. Bus stop outside park. Beach access the standout feature with unanimous agreement this is the park's greatest strength.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of children's entertainment facilities (playground, pool, activities) makes this unsuitable for families seeking on-site kid activities, losing nearly all entertainment points.
2. Severe inconsistency in cleanliness and maintenance with multiple recent reports of disgusting amenities, uncleaned facilities for days, cockroach infestations in cabins, and hot water failures indicating operational breakdown.
3. Poor value proposition with prices of $68.50-$80 per night considered excessive for minimal facilities, tight sites, and inconsistent service, with multiple reviewers specifically recommending competitors instead.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"nothing kid friendly or anything for kids to do except walk to the beach", "amenities were disgusting", "literally on the beach through an access gate in 1 minute", "Very expensive for what you get", "sites are also EXTREAMLY tight and squishy", "amenities were not cleaned on Four occasions", "cabin stunk of toilet and was infested with cockroaches and ants", "would never ever recommend this park to anyone 0/10"
